channel.title: Chris Eaton
channel.description: Thoughts on AI, building software, and the things in between.

item.title: From Passed Over to VP of Product
item.description: A year ago I was passed over for the top product role at LiveData. This month I started it. The difference was a year of building.

item.title: I Don't Write Code. My 8-Year-Old Is the PM. We Shipped Anyway.
item.description: I'm a product manager who doesn't write code. My son Harley is 8. Together we built pitchpackcards.com in a month using AI. Here's what he taught me about product.

item.title: Mud Season in Vermont: Your AI Teams Don't Need a Strategy, They Need a Road
item.description: You don't need to pave every road. You need to pave the ones your teams need to move fast on.

item.title: What Running 100 Miles Taught Me About Building AI Products
item.description: At mile 70 of the Vermont 100, everything hurts and nothing makes sense. That experience maps almost perfectly onto what it feels like to build and ship AI products.

item.title: What Building Out a Sprinter Van Taught Me About Product Management
item.description: I bought a used Sprinter 2500 and turned it into a family adventure vehicle. It was the best product management education I've had since guiding fly fishing trips.

item.title: From Ski Slopes and Rivers to Product Strategy
item.description: How working in Colorado's outdoor industry taught me everything I needed to know about product leadership.
